content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 5, 1986

Honorable David Roberti
State Senator
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
FPPC No. A-86-076

Dear Senator Roberti:

This letter is a follow-up to your letter dated
February 21, 1986, and to our subsequent telephone conversation.
You asked if you and your wife are both invited to attend a dinner
function, are you required to report on your Statement of Economic
Interests, the cost for your dinner only or the cost of both
dinners.

When you and your wife are both invited to attend a dinner
function, you are required to report only the cost of your dinner.

Please call me at (916) 322-5662, if you have any additional
questions.

Sincerely,

Alice Hughes
Technical Assistance & Analysis

AH:kt

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

STATE OF CALIFORNIA
SENATE
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

FEB 21 3 10 PM '86

DAVID ROBERTI
PRESIDENT PRO TEMPORE


February 21, 1986


Ms. Alice Hughes
Documents Examiner
Fair Political Practices
Commission
428 "J" Street, 8th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Subj: Schedule F, (Gifts) for Form 721
to be submitted March 3, 1986

Dear Ms. Hughes:

When my wife and I are both invited to a dinner function, what amount for the cost of the dinner should appear on my Form 721--the cost for my dinner or the cost for both dinners?

Sincerely,

[signature]

DAVID ROBERTI

DR/mbg
